title: China-to-Liberia Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-Series Evidence for PV Inverters in Liberia
rows.target_requirement: A Liberia-specific public EMC technical regulation for solar PV inverters has not been identified. For market acceptance, utility interconnection, donor procurement, and conformity assessment, the practical technical baseline is IEC 62920 for photovoltaic power-conversion equipment EMC, supported by applicable IEC 61000-series immunity and emissions tests. Liberia's standards authority context should be confirmed with LiSA/NSL, and grid-tied projects should confirm any LERC, LEC, or licensed-operator harmonic, flicker, immunity, and power-quality limits. Because Liberia commonly uses 60 Hz supply, EMC and harmonic evidence prepared only under China 50 Hz operating assumptions should be checked for applicability to the supplied firmware, voltage, cabling, earthing, and site conditions.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility test methods, immunity, emissions, harmonic, and flicker limits as applicable
Liberia Standards Authority (LiSA) conformity-assessment and standards-adoption context — confirm current scope before import
LERC / LEC / licensed-operator power-quality requirements — confirm project-specific harmonic and disturbance limits
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is commonly included under GB/T 37408 and related domestic tests at CNAS-accredited laboratories. The reports can reference IEC 61000-family methods, but the scope, operating voltage, 50 Hz test configuration, limits, and acceptance route are not automatically the same as IEC 62920 evidence requested by Liberia project owners or conformity bodies. A Chinese EMC certificate should therefore be mapped clause-by-clause against IEC 62920 and any LEC or donor power-quality requirement.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes domestic EMC expectations
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China GB/T EMC test evidence does not by itself prove IEC 62920 compliance for Liberia. Exporters should obtain an IEC 62920 test report or a detailed equivalence matrix from an ILAC-recognised laboratory; verify harmonic and flicker limits required by LEC or the licensed operator; confirm 60 Hz operating mode, cable lengths, earthing, surge protection, and enclosure configuration; and include high-humidity tropical installation assumptions. Where a donor, lender, or mini-grid operator specifies VeraSol, IEC TS 62257, or additional power-quality criteria, those criteria should be added to the EMC evidence package.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where specified by LiSA/NSL conformity assessment, LERC or LEC technical review, licensed-operator interconnection rules, donor procurement, lender due diligence, or project-owner specifications; otherwise best-practice technical evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — China GB/T EMC evidence supports domestic certification but is not an automatic Liberia IEC 62920 acceptance route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Chinese EMC reports as background unless they are mapped to IEC 62920 and the Liberia project's 60 Hz operating profile. Confirm LiSA/NSL conformity expectations, LERC or LEC power-quality requirements, and donor or mini-grid procurement criteria before accepting GB/T 37408 EMC evidence for Liberia.

source.publisher: Liberia Ministry of Commerce & Industry / Liberia Standards Authority (LiSA)

rows.requirement: Liberia Grid-Connection Settings — 120/240 V, 60 Hz, IEC 62116 Anti-Islanding, and IEC 61727 Interface Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Liberia is commonly reported as using a 120/240 V, 60 Hz supply system, especially around Monrovia and legacy LEC service areas influenced by US practice. Public, inverter-specific LEC interconnection settings have not been identified, so exporters should treat the 60 Hz point as a mandatory engineering confirmation rather than assume a 50 Hz African or Chinese default. Grid-tied PV inverters should be configured and documented for the actual Liberia utility or mini-grid voltage, frequency, anti-islanding trip thresholds, reconnection delay, voltage and frequency ride-through limits, and local protection scheme. IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence should be tested or reviewed at 60 Hz for the supplied firmware configuration.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
LEC or applicable local utility interconnection conditions — confirm project-specific voltage, frequency, protection, metering, and commissioning requirements
Liberia 120/240 V, 60 Hz supply context — confirm feeder-specific requirements before configuring inverter firmware
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408, and domestic anti-islanding tests. These are normally prepared around China's 220/380 V, 50 Hz grid baseline and Chinese grid-operator protection settings. Even if the hardware is capable of 60 Hz operation, China-default firmware, relay thresholds, harmonic assumptions, and anti-islanding reports cannot be assumed valid for Liberia unless the exact 60 Hz configuration is documented and, where required, retested.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
GB/T 156 — 标准电压 (Standard voltages; China 220/380 V, 50 Hz baseline)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China 50 Hz grid certificates do not prove a Liberia 60 Hz grid configuration. Exporters should obtain the feeder or mini-grid connection parameters from LEC or the relevant operator; set the inverter country code or custom profile for Liberia's actual voltage and 60 Hz frequency; document anti-islanding thresholds, reconnection timing, ride-through limits, and export-control functions; and obtain IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 evidence covering that 60 Hz firmware profile. Where a test report only covers 50 Hz China settings, require engineering justification or retesting before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for any grid-tied installation accepted by LEC or another licensed operator; exact 60 Hz settings and protection requirements are project-specific gates
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China domestic grid-review needs but does not transfer to Liberia 60 Hz acceptance without reconfiguration and review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Liberia should not be handled as a 50 Hz / 220-380 V destination by default. Treat 60 Hz grid protection as a critical compliance gap: reconfigure the inverter, document the exact firmware profile, and confirm with LEC or the relevant licensed operator whether IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence must be retested at 60 Hz.

source.publisher: Mains electricity by country compilation; verify against LEC/LERC project documents before use

rows.requirement: LERC Licensing, LEC Connection Review, and Off-Grid / Mini-Grid Solar Project Gates
rows.target_requirement: Liberia's electricity sector is regulated by the Liberia Electricity Regulatory Commission (LERC), whose public site lists licensing and permits, technical regulations, electricity licensing applications, micro-utility licensing applications, and certification of electrical professionals. LEC remains the principal utility for many grid-connected customers, while Liberia also has rural, mini-grid, and donor-funded solar electrification activity. Grid-tied PV inverters may therefore face several gates: LERC licence or permit requirements for the project activity, LEC or licensed-operator interconnection review, certified electrical-professional requirements, commissioning inspection, and donor or lender specifications. Off-grid and mini-grid projects commonly reference IEC TS 62257, VeraSol or Lighting Global quality-assurance practices, and project-specific procurement rules.
target_standards: Liberia Electricity Regulatory Commission (LERC) — licensing, permits, technical regulation, micro-utility licensing, and electrical professional certification
Liberia Electricity Corporation (LEC) or licensed operator connection requirements — confirm for each grid-tied project
IEC TS 62257 series — Recommendations for renewable energy and hybrid systems for rural electrification (common off-grid and mini-grid reference)
VeraSol / Lighting Global quality assurance frameworks — common donor procurement reference for off-grid solar equipment where specified
rows.china_equivalent: China has domestic distributed PV and utility-scale connection procedures under Chinese energy authorities and grid companies, and products may carry China voluntary certification or domestic grid-connection approvals. Those approvals do not create a Liberia project licence, LEC interconnection right, certified-installer status, or donor procurement clearance. A China factory's domestic commissioning record also does not address Liberia's low-electrification deployment context, micro-utility licensing, English documentation, local operator training, spare-parts planning, or post-installation service requirements.
china_standards: NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: China product certificates do not resolve Liberia project permissions. Before shipment, identify whether the installation is LEC-grid-tied, connected to another licensed operator, a regulated micro-utility, or fully off-grid; map the necessary LERC licence, permit, or certified-professional requirement; obtain the interconnection and commissioning conditions from LEC or the operator; and check whether donor specifications impose IEC TS 62257, VeraSol, English manuals, warranty, monitoring, cybersecurity, or service obligations. The compliance file should separate product conformity from project licensing and from local installation approval.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the project activity falls under LERC licensing, micro-utility licensing, LEC or licensed-operator interconnection, certified electrical-professional rules, or donor/lender procurement conditions
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic distributed-PV or grid-connection approvals are not transferable to LERC, LEC, licensed operators, or donor project owners in Liberia

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Liberia project approval as separate from China product certification. Confirm LERC licensing or permit requirements, LEC or operator interconnection conditions, certified electrical-professional obligations, and any donor off-grid or mini-grid specifications before committing Chinese inverter models to the project.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — Liberia Standards Authority / LERC / Project Specifications
rows.target_requirement: Liberia has an emerging standards and electricity-regulatory framework rather than a widely published, inverter-specific national type-approval rule. Recent Ministry of Commerce and Industry pages identify the Liberia Standards Authority (LiSA) as developing, adopting, and harmonising national standards and providing conformity assessment services; the older NSL naming should be confirmed with Liberia authorities for the specific import route. For PV inverters, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 safety type-test evidence is the practical project baseline used by utilities, donors, EPCs, and lenders. Where a grid-tied project also needs LERC licensing or LEC connection review, safety evidence should be tied to the exact model, firmware family, enclosure rating, and tropical operating conditions.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
Liberia Standards Authority (LiSA) / NSL naming and conformity-assessment scope — confirm current competent authority before import
Liberia Electricity Regulatory Commission (LERC) licensing and technical regulation context where the PV system is connected to a regulated electricity activity
rows.china_equivalent: China-side safety evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2019 or GB/T 37408-2021 documentation, voluntary CQC/CGC certification, and CNAS laboratory reports for the Chinese inverter model. These documents help show domestic quality control but do not automatically substitute for IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence in Liberia-facing utility, donor, or lender submissions. Chinese safety reports also need review for enclosure rating, creepage and clearance assumptions, surge category, humidity, corrosion, and temperature derating because Liberia's coastal tropical environment can be more severe than the assumptions used for a China domestic installation.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (legacy China documentation still seen in exporter files)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 safety documentation should be treated as supporting evidence only. For Liberia projects, exporters should prepare IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates or reports from an ILAC-recognised laboratory for the exact model or model family; confirm whether LiSA/NSL requires product conformity assessment at import; and confirm whether LERC, LEC, a micro-utility, or a donor project owner requires additional safety documentation. Include tropical humidity, corrosion exposure, surge protection, IP rating, and thermal derating evidence in the technical file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by LiSA/NSL conformity assessment, LERC licensing, LEC connection review, project owner procurement, donor specifications, or lender technical due diligence; otherwise best-practice safety evidence for market acceptance
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408 and NB/T 32004 evidence supports China domestic certification or procurement but does not automatically transfer to Liberia

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on China GB/T 37408 safety paperwork alone for Liberia PV inverter supply. Build an IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence file, confirm whether the competent standards body is LiSA or an NSL legacy route for the shipment, and verify any LERC, LEC, donor, or lender safety-document requirements before procurement or installation.

source.publisher: Liberia Ministry of Commerce & Industry / Liberia Standards Authority (LiSA)
